content.claim: Hungary exhibits the highest level of socioeconomic segregation between schools in the EU, with early selective tracks shown to be more beneficial for higher-status families and to increase achievement inequality, though the effect of tracking on mean achievement is contested.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Segregation measurement is national-level; specific contribution of six-/eight-year tracks vs. broader free-school-choice system not cleanly separated in cited literature
International comparative studies find inequality effect consistent but mean-achievement effect contested, requiring caution in claims about overall performance impact
Literature cited does not isolate the marginal effect of these specific track types from other selection mechanisms
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
